invariants: The two T regimes cover every real T in [1156,10000] with a shared endpoint.
No floating quadrature node, weight, or threshold is a proof input.
The compact Cauchy tail is convergent and does not use the obstructed formal full-moment tail.
The bounded T segment is not promoted to T>10000.
Fixed k=22 is not promoted to all k.
Endpoint PF, RH, Laguerre-Polya membership, and Lambda <= 0 are forbidden as inputs.

matrix_rows.claim: Split the finite T segment at T=15625/8: use fixed x_*=8/25 below the transition and fixed y_*=200 above it.
Absolute full-Gamma moments bound every degree-0..384 compact Taylor contribution uniformly in T after the value T^3 and derivative T^2 scalings.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Compact polynomial majorant for four indices on the finite T segment only.

matrix_rows.claim: The common |z|<=0.38 Cauchy disk gives scaled degree-384 remainders uniformly across both T regimes.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Convergent compact Cauchy remainder only; not formal full-moment tail summation.

matrix_rows.claim: Endpoint-monotone finite-Phi majorants and upper incomplete-Gamma moments bound the real tails uniformly in each T regime.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite n<=30 real-tail majorants on 1156<=T<=10000 only.

matrix_rows.claim: The global n>=31 numerator and Phi(0) correction remains negligible after worst-case T^3 and T^2 scaling on the finite segment.

uniform_residual_rows.proof_boundary: Uniform residual majorant on 1156<=T<=10000 for one F_i only; not T>10000.

matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Four-index residual theorem on the bounded real-T segment only; not T>10000.

matrix_rows.claim: The exact rational residual budgets and Arb perturbation ledger certify the fixed-k=22 normalizers and three stencil inequalities for every 1156<=T<=10000.

perturbation_rows.name: normalizer
perturbation_rows.proof_boundary: Arb perturbation inequality for the rational A,B budgets on 0<=u<=1/1156.

matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Fixed k=22 on 1156<=T<=10000 only; not the unbounded collar or all k.

matrix_rows.claim: The bounded segment certificate proves the same residual and stencil bounds for every T>10000.
matrix_rows.gap: The crude normalization and fixed-degree Cauchy bounds were scaled only through T=10000; an asymptotic or adaptive split is still required on the unbounded ray.

matrix_rows.claim: The fixed-k=22 segment certificate proves cone entry for all k.
matrix_rows.gap: Only F_21 through F_24 and one k-window are controlled.

matrix_rows.claim: Promotion requires a certified T>10000 tail and all required k windows; neither finite endpoints nor RH-assuming inputs may replace them.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Proof-hygiene gate only; not cone entry, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

proof_boundary: Uniform fixed-k=22 residual and stencil certificate on the bounded real interval 1156<=T<=10000 only. It does not cover T>10000, does not cover all k, does not prove the full cone-entry or sign-regularity theorem, and does not prove RH or Lambda <= 0.

summary.main_finding: A two-regime exact-moment/Cauchy and incomplete-Gamma majorant proves the rational residual budgets uniformly for every real 1156<=T<=10000 and i=21..24. Composed with the Arb degree-40 perturbation ledger, this certifies the complete fixed-k=22 stencil system on the whole bounded collar segment. The unbounded ray T>10000, remaining k coverage, cone entry, RH, and Lambda <= 0 remain open.
